name: HoneyDrunk.Kernel
description: Context propagation, lifecycle orchestration, and Grid primitives that power every Node across the Hive. The foundational runtime layer.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Kernel is the semantic OS layer of HoneyDrunk.OS, the Hive. It is the foundational runtime that nodes, agents and services use to communicate, coordinate and observe themselves across the Grid. Core responsibilities: Context propagation—a three tier model (GridContext, NodeContext, OperationContext) that flows across async boundaries and transports. Lifecycle orchestration—startup hooks, lifecycle stages, health and readiness aggregation, graceful shutdown. Configuration and identity primitives—strongly typed IDs at configuration time, string based identities at runtime, options for Grid and Node bootstrap. Telemetry integration—OpenTelemetry ready activity factory, trace enrichment and log correlation wired to Grid context. Health and readiness—health checks and lifecycle contributors for Kubernetes style /health and /ready endpoints. Agent interop—agent execution contexts, scoped context visibility and serializers that let higher level agent runtimes safely embed LLMs into the Grid. Kernel is the part of the Grid that defines what it means to be a node.
long_description.why_it_exists: To give every node the same runtime skeleton for identity, context, lifecycle and observability so those concerns are not reinvented in each service.
long_description.primary_audience: Node developers and service owners building Grid aware services. Agent runtimes and automation that need to execute with a proper Grid context.
value_props: Three tier context model—Grid to Node to Operation—with correlation and causation tracking
Lifecycle orchestration with startup hooks, lifecycle stages and contributor based health and readiness
OpenTelemetry ready tracing, enrichment and log scopes
Strongly typed identity at configuration time, string identity at runtime for performance
Kubernetes friendly health and readiness endpoints
Agent aware execution contexts and context serializers for LLM centric components
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal first. Public SDK patterns may be open core, but Kernel itself is the base runtime that all higher layers depend on.
long_description.roadmap_focus: v0.4 is a stabilization release. Enforce the three-ID context model across all creation paths. Consolidate hosting around AddHoneyDrunkNode as the canonical API. Align lifecycle, health and readiness behavior with docs. Lock identity registries as the DNS of the Grid.
long_description.grid_relationship: Foundation for all Core nodes. Every node depends on Kernel or Kernel.Abstractions.
long_description.demo_path: Minimal Node setup with AddHoneyDrunkGrid and AddHoneyDrunkNode → context flow across async calls → health probe validation → graceful shutdown with lifecycle hooks.

name: HoneyDrunk.Transport
description: Unified messaging abstraction over Azure Service Bus, Storage Queue, and in-memory brokers. Features middleware pipeline pattern, retry strategies, transactional outbox, and Grid context propagation.

long_description.overview: Transport is the messaging backbone for HoneyDrunk.OS applications. It provides transport-agnostic abstractions (ITransportPublisher, ITransportConsumer, IMessageHandler<T>) that enable broker-independent messaging. Built on an onion-style middleware pipeline similar to ASP.NET Core, it supports GridContext propagation, OpenTelemetry tracing, structured logging, and configurable retry strategies (Fixed, Linear, Exponential). The transactional outbox pattern ensures exactly-once delivery by storing messages within database transactions. Transport adapters for Azure Service Bus, Azure Storage Queue, and InMemory are available as separate packages.
long_description.why_it_exists: HoneyDrunk.Transport exists to decouple application logic from specific message broker implementations while providing enterprise-grade reliability patterns.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk service developers building distributed, event-driven applications.
value_props: Transport-agnostic messaging via ITransportPublisher/ITransportConsumer
Middleware pipeline for cross-cutting concerns (telemetry, logging, correlation)
Configurable retry strategies with backoff (Fixed, Linear, Exponential)
Transactional outbox pattern for exactly-once delivery
Grid context propagation with correlation and causation tracking
Health contributors for Kubernetes readiness/liveness probes
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation; enterprise patterns emerge in implementations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advanced dead-letter strategies, per-subscription filters, and fault injection for testing.
long_description.grid_relationship: Uses Kernel's IGridContext, IOperationContext, and ITelemetryActivityFactory for correlation and telemetry. Provides Outbox contracts that Data bridges can implement.
long_description.demo_path: Publish a message via IMessagePublisher, observe middleware pipeline execution, verify handler invocation with Grid context.

name: HoneyDrunk.Vault
description: Secure secrets and configuration management. Zero-trust vault for sensitive data across environments.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Vault is the core secrets and configuration management library for HoneyDrunk.OS. This package provides the abstractions (ISecretStore, IConfigProvider, IVaultClient, ISecretProvider, IConfigSource), caching with TTL and sliding expiration, resilience policies (retry, circuit breaker), Kernel lifecycle integration, and distributed telemetry that all Vault providers plug into. Vault itself contains no provider implementations—it defines the contract, runtime behavior, and orchestration. Providers (File, Azure Key Vault, AWS, InMemory, Configuration) supply the actual secrets while Vault handles resilience, caching, and tracing.
long_description.why_it_exists: A unified, Kernel-aware interface for secrets and configuration—no matter where those values live. Prevents inconsistent secret handling and reinvention across the Grid.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk service developers and node owners who need secure, traceable access to secrets and configuration.
value_props: Defines ISecretStore, IConfigProvider, IVaultClient for unified secret/config access
In-memory caching with TTL and optional sliding expiration
Retry and circuit breaker resilience policies
Grid context propagation for tracing and correlation
Kernel lifecycle integration (startup warmup, health, readiness)
Secure telemetry (never logs secret values)
Pluggable provider model (File, Azure, AWS, InMemory, Configuration)
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; open-core where appropriate.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Pulse integration for anomaly detection, secrets-as-code manifests, autonomous credential rotation.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: honeydrunk-kernel, honeydrunk-auth, honeysentinel, breachlab-exe.
long_description.demo_path: Register Vault with AddVault() → retrieve a secret via ISecretStore → observe caching, resilience, and telemetry.

name: HoneyDrunk.Vault.Rotation
description: Azure Function App that rotates third-party provider secrets (Resend, Twilio, OpenAI, ...) into per-Node Key Vaults on schedule. Sibling to HoneyDrunk.Vault; separate deployable.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Vault.Rotation is a deployable Azure Function Node for ADR-0006 Tier-2 secret rotation. It rotates third-party provider credentials on schedule and writes new versions into per-Node Key Vaults.
long_description.why_it_exists: Centralize third-party key rotation and SLA enforcement without duplicating rotator infrastructure in every Node.
long_description.primary_audience: Platform maintainers operating HoneyDrunk secret lifecycle automation.
value_props: Automated Tier-2 provider secret rotation
Per-vault write model aligned with Key Vault RBAC
Shared telemetry/alerting hooks for SLA monitoring
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal platform capability.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Scaffold baseline Function runtime, then integrate provider adapters and observability.
long_description.grid_relationship: Depends on Kernel and Vault, writes rotated values into deployable Node vaults.
long_description.demo_path: Trigger rotation run → write new secret version → observe downstream refresh event path.

name: HoneyDrunk.Auth
description: Grid node component for authentication and authorization. Provides JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys, deterministic policy evaluation, and deep Kernel integration for lifecycle and telemetry.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Auth is a Grid node component—not a standalone library—that standardizes authentication and authorization decisions across nodes and entrypoints. It requires Kernel as its host runtime and provides JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys, deterministic policy evaluation, and fail-fast startup validation. Auth ensures consistent identity checks and authorization patterns so every request entering the Grid follows the same security discipline.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents inconsistent authentication and authorization patterns across nodes, ensuring security discipline is uniform regardless of entrypoint.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners building HTTP APIs, gateways, and any Grid node requiring request authentication.
value_props: JWT bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys
Deterministic policy evaluation (local, side-effect free, auditable)
Fail-fast startup validation of required secrets
Deep Kernel integration for lifecycle hooks, telemetry, and context propagation
Clean separation between auth contracts (Abstractions) and implementations
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation; enterprise patterns in implementations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Passkeys and MFA support. Federated sign-ins and device trust. Pulse integration for session observability.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (required) for GridContext, OperationContext, lifecycle hooks, and telemetry. Consumes Vault (required) for signing keys and auth configuration. Exposes auth contracts consumed by Web.Rest.
long_description.demo_path: Validate a JWT bearer token and evaluate an authorization policy with full telemetry tracing.

name: HoneyDrunk.Web.Rest
description: Standardized REST API response envelopes, middleware for correlation/exception handling, and conventions for consistent API behavior across services.

long_description.overview: Web.Rest provides two packages: Abstractions (zero-dependency contracts for ApiResult, ApiErrorResponse, pagination, error codes) and AspNetCore (middleware for correlation ID propagation, exception-to-error mapping, auth failure shaping, and MVC/minimal API conventions). Together they ensure every REST API in the Grid returns consistent response envelopes with proper correlation tracking.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents inconsistent error shapes, missing correlation IDs, and divergent response formats across services. One AddRest()/UseRest() call configures everything.
long_description.primary_audience: ASP.NET Core service developers building REST APIs in the HoneyDrunk ecosystem.
value_props: Standardized response envelopes (ApiResult<T>, ApiErrorResponse)
Automatic correlation ID propagation across requests
Exception-to-structured-error mapping middleware
Auth failure shaping (401/403 as JSON, not redirects)
Zero-dependency Abstractions package for shared contracts
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation for REST consistency.
long_description.roadmap_focus: OpenAPI enrichment, typed client generation, WebSocket conventions.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel.Abstractions (IOperationContextAccessor for correlation), Auth.AspNetCore (auth shaping), Transport.Abstractions (envelope mapping only).
long_description.demo_path: Call AddRest() → UseRest() → all responses automatically include correlation IDs and structured errors.

name: HoneyDrunk.Data
description: Persistence contracts, EF Core and SQL Server provider implementations, transactional outbox with lease-based dispatch, migrations discipline, tenant isolation, and diagnostics.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Data gives the Grid memory and continuity. It defines persistence contracts and provider conventions across every tenant, every node, and every environment, so storage stays predictable and auditable. This Node standardizes migrations, schema versioning, tenant isolation, and persistence telemetry. It includes a transactional outbox pattern with lease-based concurrency for exactly-once message delivery.
long_description.why_it_exists: HoneyDrunk.Data exists to prevent inconsistency and reinvention across the Grid.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk maintainers, contributors, and service owners.
value_props: Standardizes database, migrations, and persistence patterns
Transactional outbox with lease-based concurrency for exactly-once delivery
Reduces operational risk with opinionated defaults
Improves developer velocity and interoperability
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; open-core where appropriate.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advance HoneyDrunk.Data capabilities and harden contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (required) for GridContext, OperationContext, lifecycle hooks, and telemetry primitives. Consumes Transport (via Outbox.Dispatcher) for message publishing.
long_description.demo_path: Register Data with AddHoneyDrunkData() → use repositories with tenant isolation → verify outbox delivery.

name: Pulse
description: Unified observability. Logs, traces, and metrics in one interface. See the heartbeat of The Hive.

long_description.overview: Pulse is how the Hive feels itself. It's the nervous system that collects, correlates, and interprets telemetry from every corner of the Grid — logs, traces, metrics, and signals that together form a living heartbeat. Every Node reports its state into Pulse's unified pipeline, creating a continuously updated map of operational health and system interdependence.
long_description.why_it_exists: In distributed systems, invisibility is the enemy. Pulse exists to prevent blindness.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk maintainers, contributors, and service owners.
value_props: Standardizes observability and monitoring
Reduces operational risk with opinionated defaults
Improves developer velocity and interoperability
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; open-core where appropriate.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advance Pulse capabilities and harden contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: honeydrunk-vault, honeydrunk-transport, honeydrunk-data.
long_description.demo_path: Configure Pulse sinks → emit telemetry from a Node → observe logs, traces, and metrics in unified dashboard.

name: HoneyDrunk.Notify
description: Channel-agnostic notification dispatch system with multi-provider email (SMTP, Resend), SMS (Twilio), template rendering, and queue-based async delivery.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Notify is the Grid's outbound notification layer — a channel-agnostic system for dispatching messages across email, SMS, and future channels. It provides a unified contract surface (INotificationSender, INotificationGateway, INotificationPolicy) with pluggable providers: SMTP and Resend for email, Twilio for SMS. Template rendering, idempotency keys, delivery tracking, and queue-based async dispatch ensure reliable, auditable delivery.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without unified notification infrastructure, outbound communication drifts in reliability, security, and delivery patterns across nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers and node owners needing reliable, multi-channel outbound notifications with consistent delivery semantics.
value_props: Channel-agnostic dispatch via INotificationSender and INotificationGateway
Multi-provider email (SMTP, Resend) and SMS (Twilio) with pluggable architecture
Template rendering with ITemplateRenderer and IEmailTemplateRenderer
Queue-based async delivery with background worker processing
Idempotency keys and delivery status tracking
Secure credential management via deploy-time Key Vault injection
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; provider ecosystem may expand to push notifications, webhooks, and partner integrations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Push notification channel, webhook dispatch, adaptive send policies based on Pulse metrics.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel for Grid context, lifecycle hooks, and telemetry. Provider credentials are injected at deploy time via Azure Key Vault (not the HoneyDrunk.Vault package).
long_description.demo_path: Send a notification via INotificationSender → provider dispatches email/SMS → track delivery status.
long_description.signal_quote: When the Hive speaks, it's heard everywhere.

name: HoneyDrunk.Communications
description: Decision and orchestration layer for outbound communications. Determines why, when, and to whom messages are sent. Manages multi-step flows, campaigns, user preferences, suppression, cadence rules, and business-event-to-message-intent mapping. Delegates all delivery mechanics to Notify.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Communications is the brain behind outbound messaging. While Notify handles the mechanics of delivery (render, send, retry, track), Communications owns the decision layer: should a message be sent, to whom, when, and as part of what workflow. It maps business events (user signed up, subscription expiring, agent completed task) to message intents, resolves recipients and their preferences, enforces suppression and cadence rules, and orchestrates multi-step communication flows (welcome sequences, re-engagement campaigns, escalation chains). Communications calls Notify's INotificationSender for every outbound message but never touches provider adapters, templates, or retry logic directly.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without a dedicated orchestration layer, message-sending logic scatters across every Node that needs to communicate with users. That leads to duplicated preference checks, inconsistent cadence enforcement, and no single place to reason about a user's full communication lifecycle.
long_description.primary_audience: Node developers who need to trigger user-facing communications from business events. Product and growth workflows that require multi-step messaging sequences.
value_props: Business-event-to-message-intent mapping — declare what happened, Communications decides what to send
Recipient resolution with preference and suppression awareness
Cadence rules — rate-limit and space out messages per user
Multi-step flows — welcome sequences, drip campaigns, escalation chains
Clean separation from delivery mechanics — Communications never touches SMTP or Twilio
Auditable decision log — every send-or-suppress decision is recorded with reasoning
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Campaign and lifecycle messaging capabilities may surface in product offerings.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Define core abstractions (intent mapping, preference resolution, flow engine). First implementation: welcome email sequence using Notify as delivery backend.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel for Grid context and lifecycle. Consumes Notify's INotificationSender for all delivery. May consume Data for preference and flow state persistence. Pulse receives telemetry on communication decisions.
long_description.demo_path: Fire a UserSignedUp event → Communications resolves intent (welcome email) → checks preferences → calls Notify → records decision.

name: HoneyDrunk.Actions
description: Public CI toolkit for OSS repos. Mirrors contracts with Pipelines while staying feed-agnostic.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Actions brings HoneyDrunk's engineering discipline to the open world. It's the public CI/CD framework for GitHub repositories, mirroring the rigor of Pipelines but tuned for transparency and collaboration. It standardizes testing, packaging, and deployment for public projects without depending on private infrastructure.
long_description.why_it_exists: Consistent CI/CD patterns across all public GitHub repos, preventing reinvention of build and test workflows.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk maintainers, contributors, and service owners.
value_props: Standardizes GitHub Actions workflows
Reduces operational risk with opinionated defaults
Improves developer velocity and interoperability
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first; open-core where appropriate.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advance HoneyDrunk.Actions capabilities and harden contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Connects with: honeydrunk-build.
long_description.demo_path: Reference a reusable workflow → observe build, test, pack, publish steps → verify artifacts.

name: HoneyDrunk.Architecture
description: Canonical architecture knowledge base — ADRs, catalogs, routing rules, issue templates, and agent workflows that govern how the Grid evolves.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Architecture is the command center of the Grid. It holds the canonical catalogs (nodes, modules, services, relationships), constitution (manifesto, invariants, sector taxonomy), architecture decision records, routing rules for agent workflows, and issue/sync packet generation. No code, no packages — pure knowledge infrastructure that agents and humans use to understand and evolve the Hive.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without a single source of architectural truth, decisions scatter across repos, agents lack context, and the Grid drifts.
long_description.primary_audience: Architecture agents, Copilot workflows, and HoneyDrunk maintainers making cross-cutting decisions.
value_props: Single source of truth for Grid topology and node catalogs
Architecture Decision Records with full context and cascade analysis
Agent routing rules that direct work to the correct repos
Issue and site-sync packet generation for cross-repo coordination
Constitution and invariants that define Grid-wide rules
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal governance infrastructure.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Converge with website data. Expand agent workflows. Add initiative tracking and roadmap generation.
long_description.grid_relationship: Referenced by all agents and copilot instructions. No runtime dependencies.
long_description.demo_path: Ask the ADR Composer agent a trade-off question → observe context loading from catalogs → review the generated ADR.
long_description.signal_quote: Know the map. Tend the garden.

name: HoneyDrunk.Studios
description: The live front-end of The Hive — renders all Nodes, Sectors, and signals with neon-lit visualization. Next.js 16, React 19, Three.js WebGL.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Studios is the public face of the Hive — a Next.js 16 application that renders the Grid as an interactive, neon-lit visualization. It displays every Node, Sector, signal timeline entry, and relationship using static JSON data imported directly (no CMS, no database). The flagship /grid route renders an interactive Three.js WebGL scene. The site serves as both a developer portal and a build-in-public showcase.
long_description.why_it_exists: To make the state of The Grid observable to contributors and the community. Transparency as a feature.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers, contributors, and anyone tracking HoneyDrunk's build-in-public roadmap.
value_props: Interactive 3D Grid visualization via Three.js
Real-time reflection of architecture state via static JSON
Signal timeline showing build-in-public progress
Sector and Node deep-dive pages with rich descriptions
Deployed on Vercel with automatic previews
long_description.monetization_signal: Public-facing brand and developer relations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Converge data with Architecture repo. Add live status indicators. Expand module and service detail pages.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Architecture catalogs as source of truth for data/schema files. No runtime Node dependencies.
long_description.demo_path: Visit honeydrunkstudios.com → explore /grid WebGL → browse /nodes → check /signal timeline.

name: HoneyDrunk.AI
description: Normalized inference contracts for chat completion, embeddings, and structured output. Provider adapters for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, and local models. Token/latency telemetry on every call.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.AI is the inference abstraction layer for the Grid. It provides normalized contracts for chat completion, text completion, embeddings, and structured output across multiple model providers. Every inference call emits Pulse telemetry with token counts, latency, model identifiers, and cost estimates. Provider adapters wrap Microsoft.Extensions.AI implementations with Grid context enrichment, Pulse telemetry, and Vault-backed credential resolution.
long_description.why_it_exists: Model providers change frequently. A provider swap should be a config change, not an agent rewrite. Centralizing inference behind normalized contracts prevents provider lock-in and ensures consistent telemetry.
long_description.primary_audience: AI sector Nodes (Agents, Evals, Sim) and any Grid service that needs model inference.
value_props: Normalized inference contracts aligned with Microsoft.Extensions.AI
Pluggable provider adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, local/ONNX)
Automatic token/latency/cost telemetry via Pulse
Vault-backed API key management
Model selection and routing based on capability requirements
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Provider abstraction enables cost optimization across models.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Initial scaffolding with OpenAI and Anthropic providers. Align with Microsoft.Extensions.AI adoption.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, telemetry), Vault (API keys), Pulse (inference telemetry). Consumed by Agents, Memory, Knowledge, Evals, Sim.
long_description.demo_path: Register AI with AddHoneyDrunkAI() → configure an OpenAI provider → run a chat completion → observe Pulse telemetry with token counts and latency.
long_description.signal_quote: Think clearly, speak in any voice.

name: HoneyDrunk.Capabilities
description: Tool definitions, discovery, permissioning, versioning, and execution dispatch for agents. The capability layer that connects agents to the rest of the Grid.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Capabilities is the tool registry and dispatch layer for the Grid's agents. It defines what tools exist, what parameters they accept, what they return, and who is authorized to invoke them. Tool implementations live in the Node that owns the domain (e.g., a 'query database' tool is implemented by Data). Capabilities owns discovery, schema versioning, permission checks, and execution routing.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without a centralized tool registry, agent-tool bindings couple agent code to tool implementations. Capabilities decouples them — agents discover tools at runtime, and tool updates don't require agent redeployment.
long_description.primary_audience: Agent developers and Node owners who expose domain operations as agent-callable tools.
value_props: Centralized tool registry with schema definitions
Runtime discovery — agents query available tools dynamically
Permission guards — which agents can invoke which tools
Schema versioning — tool contracts evolve without breaking consumers
Execution dispatch — route invocations to implementing Nodes
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Tool ecosystem becomes a platform capability.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core registry contracts, permission model, initial tool descriptors for Data and Vault operations.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, identity), Auth (authorization policies). Consumed by Agents (tool invocation).
long_description.demo_path: Register a tool descriptor → agent discovers it via ICapabilityRegistry → invoke with permission check → observe dispatch to implementing Node.
long_description.signal_quote: Reach for the right tool, every time.

name: HoneyDrunk.Agents
description: Agent lifecycle management, execution context, tool and memory interfaces, orchestration hooks, and agent identity. The runtime that brings agents to life in the Grid.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Agents is the agent runtime for the Grid. It manages the full agent lifecycle (register → initialize → execute → complete → decommission), provides scoped execution contexts carrying GridContext, agent identity, memory references, and capability bindings. Agents defines the contracts for how agents invoke tools and access memory, with orchestration hooks for the Flow Node to coordinate multi-agent sequences.
long_description.why_it_exists: Agents need a consistent runtime skeleton — just as Kernel gives every Node context propagation and lifecycle hooks, Agents gives every AI agent identity, execution context, and tool/memory access patterns.
long_description.primary_audience: AI workflow developers building agents that operate within the Grid.
value_props: Full agent lifecycle management with defined state transitions
Scoped execution contexts with GridContext propagation
Tool interfaces via IToolInvoker resolved through Capabilities
Memory interfaces via IAgentMemory for read/write during execution
Orchestration hooks for multi-agent coordination via Flow
Agent identity with AgentId, capability declarations, and authorization scope
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Agent runtime is the execution engine for all AI-driven features.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core lifecycle contracts, execution context, tool invoker integration with Capabilities.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, lifecycle, identity), AI (inference), Capabilities (tool registry). Consumed by Flow (agent chaining), HoneyHub (agent execution surface).
long_description.demo_path: Register an agent → trigger execution → observe lifecycle stages → agent calls a tool via IToolInvoker → writes memory → completes.

name: HoneyDrunk.Memory
description: Persistent and contextual memory system for agents. Short-term (conversation-scoped) and long-term (cross-execution) memory with scoped isolation, indexing, and summarization.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Memory provides persistent and contextual memory for Grid agents. It manages short-term memory (conversation-scoped, cleared after execution) and long-term memory (persists across executions, scoped by tenant/project/agent). Memory storage, retrieval, search, and forgetting are all contract-driven with pluggable backends. Indexing and summarization compress memories for efficient retrieval at scale.
long_description.why_it_exists: Agents without memory are stateless — every execution starts from scratch. Memory gives agents continuity, learning, and personalization across interactions.
long_description.primary_audience: Agent developers who need agents to remember context across executions.
value_props: Short-term and long-term memory with clear lifecycle semantics
Scoped isolation — TenantId/ProjectId/AgentId boundaries enforced at storage level
Pluggable backends (SQL Server, Cosmos DB, InMemory)
Automatic summarization to compress old memories
Similarity search via embeddings from HoneyDrunk.AI
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Memory backends may have tiered storage costs at scale.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core contracts (IMemoryStore, IMemoryScope), InMemory provider, integration with Agents execution context.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, scoping), Data (persistence patterns), AI (embeddings for similarity search). Consumed by Agents (memory read/write during execution).
long_description.demo_path: Agent writes a memory during execution → agent completes → new execution reads prior memories → observes scoped isolation.
long_description.signal_quote: What was learned is never lost.

name: HoneyDrunk.Knowledge
description: Document ingestion, chunking, embedding, retrieval pipelines (RAG), source attribution, and knowledge versioning. The Grid's interface to external information.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Knowledge is the Grid's interface to external information. It ingests documents, web content, API responses, and structured data, then chunks and embeds them for retrieval. RAG pipelines return ranked results with full source attribution. Knowledge versioning ensures retrieval reflects the current state of ingested sources. Supports both traditional RAG (chunk/embed/search) and structured knowledge surfaces (LLM-maintained markdown wikis with auto-indexes).
long_description.why_it_exists: Agents need access to information beyond their training data and conversation context. Knowledge provides a structured, versioned, attributable interface to external information.
long_description.primary_audience: Agent developers, workflow builders, and applications that need grounded AI responses.
value_props: Document ingestion with pluggable parsers
Chunking and embedding via HoneyDrunk.AI
RAG retrieval pipelines with ranked, attributed results
Source attribution — every chunk traces to its origin
Knowledge versioning — sources update; retrieval reflects current state
Pluggable backends (Azure AI Search, Postgres pgvector, InMemory)
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Knowledge infrastructure enables premium AI features.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core contracts (IKnowledgeStore, IDocumentIngester, IRetrievalPipeline), InMemory provider, basic markdown ingestion.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context), AI (embeddings), Data (persistence patterns). Consumed by Agents (knowledge retrieval), Lore (knowledge compilation), HoneyHub (context for planning).
long_description.demo_path: Ingest a set of markdown files → query with a natural language question → receive ranked results with source attribution.

name: HoneyDrunk.Flow
description: Multi-step workflow definitions, long-running process management, retry and compensation, agent chaining, state persistence, and checkpoint/resume with human-in-the-loop approval.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Flow is the execution-level workflow engine for the Grid. It manages multi-step workflow definitions (sequences, branches, parallel execution), long-running processes that span minutes to days, retry with backoff, compensation logic for failed steps, and agent chaining where one agent's output feeds the next. Workflows persist state across process restarts and can pause for human approval via Operator.
long_description.why_it_exists: Single-agent, single-step execution covers simple tasks. Real-world AI work requires multi-step pipelines, error recovery, human checkpoints, and agent coordination. Flow provides this without coupling orchestration logic into agent code.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers building multi-step AI workflows, agent pipelines, and long-running automated processes.
value_props: Declarative workflow definitions with steps, transitions, and compensation
Long-running process management with persistent state
Retry strategies with configurable backoff
Agent chaining — output of one agent feeds the next
Checkpoint and resume for human-in-the-loop approval via Operator
Saga pattern with compensation for distributed rollback
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Workflow orchestration enables complex product features.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core contracts (IWorkflow, IWorkflowEngine, IWorkflowStep), simple sequential execution, state persistence via Data.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, lifecycle), Agents (agent execution), Data (state persistence), Transport (async step coordination). Consumed by HoneyHub (workflow triggering).
long_description.demo_path: Define a two-step workflow → first step runs an agent → second step runs on agent output → observe state persistence and telemetry.

name: HoneyDrunk.Operator
description: Approval gates, safety controls, circuit breakers, cost controls, incident intervention, audit trail, and decision authority for AI operations. The human control plane.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Operator is the human control plane for the Hive. It provides approval gates for actions requiring sign-off, safety controls for content filtering and output validation, circuit breakers for emergency stops, cost controls with per-agent/per-workflow budgets, and an immutable audit trail of every AI decision, tool invocation, and human override. Operator is the only Node with authority to halt other AI Nodes.
long_description.why_it_exists: The system that decides what to do must never be the system that decides whether it's allowed to do it. Operator provides independent, externally deployable safety controls that don't depend on agent runtime correctness.
long_description.primary_audience: Platform operators, security teams, and anyone responsible for AI safety and cost governance.
value_props: Approval gates — require human sign-off before high-risk actions proceed
Circuit breakers — kill switches for agents, inference, or entire workflows
Cost controls — budget limits per agent, per workflow, per time window
Safety filters — validate outputs before they leave the system
Immutable audit trail of all AI decisions and human overrides
Decision policies — auto-approve, auto-deny, or require-human based on rules
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Enterprise AI governance is a premium capability.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core contracts (IApprovalGate, ICircuitBreaker, ICostGuard, IAuditLog), basic policy engine.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (context, identity), Auth (authorization), Data (audit persistence), Pulse (operational telemetry). Consumed by Flow (approval gates), Agents (action constraints), HoneyHub (decision authority surface).
long_description.demo_path: Set a cost budget → agent exceeds it → Operator halts execution → observe audit trail entry.

name: HoneyDrunk.Evals
description: Prompt evaluation, regression testing, model comparison, output quality scoring, and versioned evaluation datasets. The quality gate for AI behavior.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Evals is the quality layer for AI behavior in the Grid. It runs prompts against models, scores outputs against expected criteria (factuality, relevance, safety, format compliance), detects regressions when models or prompts change, and compares performance across multiple providers. Evaluation results are emitted as Pulse signals so HoneyHub can correlate quality metrics to specific deployments or model changes.
long_description.why_it_exists: Model upgrades and prompt changes can silently degrade output quality. Evals provides automated regression detection so quality issues are caught before they reach production.
long_description.primary_audience: AI developers and prompt engineers who need confidence that changes don't break existing behavior.
value_props: Run evaluation suites against any model/provider
Structured scoring rubrics (factuality, relevance, safety, format)
Regression detection across model upgrades and prompt changes
Model comparison — same eval set against multiple providers
Versioned evaluation datasets
Pulse integration for quality signals and alerting
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Eval infrastructure enables confident model upgrades.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Core contracts (IEvaluator, IEvalDataset, IEvalScorer), basic model-as-judge scorer, Pulse signal emission.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes AI (inference for running evaluations), Pulse (emitting eval metrics). Consumed by HoneyHub (quality correlation to features).
long_description.demo_path: Define an eval dataset → run against two models → compare scores → observe Pulse signals for regression detection.

name: HoneyDrunk.Sim
description: Scenario modeling, plan evaluation, risk analysis, and pre-execution validation. Preview actions before committing to real execution.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Sim provides simulation and planning capabilities for the Grid. It models hypothetical scenarios and simulates outcomes, evaluates proposed workflows or agent actions before real execution, identifies failure modes and cost exposure, and validates plans against simulated state. Early implementation uses rule-based risk scoring; mature implementation uses model-based simulation.
long_description.why_it_exists: When agents operate autonomously, the ability to preview actions before committing is a safety multiplier. Sim catches expensive mistakes before they happen.
long_description.primary_audience: Platform operators and workflow designers who need confidence before committing to real execution.
value_props: Scenario modeling with projected outcomes
Plan evaluation — estimate results before executing
Risk analysis — failure modes, probabilities, mitigations
Pre-execution validation — dry-run against simulated state
Graduated implementation from rule-based to model-based
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal-first. Simulation is a premium enterprise safety feature.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Optional for initial AI sector delivery. Core contracts (ISimulator, IScenario, IRiskAssessment) when prioritized.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes AI (inference for simulation), Knowledge (context for scenarios), Agents (agent behavior models).
long_description.demo_path: Define a scenario → run simulation → review projected outcomes and risk assessment → decide whether to proceed with real execution.

name: HoneyDrunk.Lore
description: Flat-file wiki maintained by LLMs. Ingests raw sources, compiles structured markdown, self-maintains indexes and backlinks, answers queries, and runs health checks. Consumed by agents and the solo developer as the ecosystem's compiled research surface.

long_description.overview: HoneyDrunk.Lore is the Hive's living knowledge surface — an LLM-compiled wiki that continuously ingests raw sources (articles, papers, repos, datasets, images), compiles them into structured markdown with auto-maintained indexes, backlinks, concept articles, and summaries. It runs health checks to find inconsistencies, impute missing data, and suggest new article candidates. Queries return rendered markdown, slide shows, or visualizations, and outputs are filed back into the wiki to enhance it for further queries. The human rarely edits the wiki directly — it is the domain of the LLM.
long_description.why_it_exists: Architecture holds governance decisions. Studios holds the public face. Lore holds the Hive's accumulated knowledge — everything the ecosystem has learned, researched, and compiled. A living knowledge base that grows smarter with every interaction.
long_description.primary_audience: HoneyDrunk Studios (internal research and knowledge), AI agents needing ecosystem context, and anyone querying the Hive's accumulated knowledge.
value_props: Raw source ingestion into structured, browsable markdown wiki
LLM-compiled with auto-maintained indexes, backlinks, and concept maps
Q&A over accumulated knowledge with rendered outputs (markdown, slides, images)
Self-maintaining — health checks, consistency linting, gap detection
Outputs filed back into wiki for compound knowledge growth
Viewable in Obsidian as a living, navigable knowledge surface
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal knowledge infrastructure. Product potential as a standalone knowledge base tool.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Initial scaffolding — raw/ ingestion, LLM compilation pipeline, index maintenance, basic Q&A workflows.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Knowledge (ingestion infrastructure, retrieval), Agents (compilation and Q&A agents), AI (inference for compilation and queries), Flow (multi-step compilation workflows). Lives in Meta sector alongside Architecture and Studios.
long_description.demo_path: Ingest raw sources into raw/ → LLM compiles into wiki/ → browse in Obsidian → ask a complex question → receive rendered answer filed back into wiki.
